iterations.theme: Reflections on Retirement, Identity, Church Challenges, and the Role of Faith in Modern Society
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration covers the entire podcast, as its duration (approx. 42 minutes) falls within the specified range for a single thematic block, and the conversation flows naturally from introduction to conclusion without a major thematic shift requiring a split.

chapters.topic: Introduction of Don Anton Šuljić and his diverse career
Don Anton's retirement and focus on writing unpublished books
The significance of 'homeland' (zavičaj) and island identity
Reflections on his professional roles, especially teaching theology
His stance on the pandemic and reliance on scientific/church authorities
Criticism of Pope Francis and the erosion of trust in authority
Pope Francis: Continuity vs. Discontinuity, and the 'preferential option for the poor'
Breakdown of dialogue in the Church due to clericalization and bureaucracy
Critique of church media and the Church's diminishing social relevance
Progress and shortcomings of the Church in post-communist Croatia
